title: vicia graminea var. bidentata
intro: Vicia graminea var. bidentata, also known as the two-toothed vetch, is a perennial legume plant native to Europe. This plant is used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, including its diuretic, antispasmodic, and anti-inflammatory effects. The two-toothed vetch is said to have various health benefits, including relieving urinary tract issues, soothing digestive problems, and reducing menstrual cramps. The plant can be prepared in herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are commonly consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
